frustrated.definition: feeling annoyed because you cannot change something
frustrated.example: He was frustrated that he could not fix the problem.

cling.definition: to hold on tightly to something and not let go
cling.example: The child would cling to her mother's hand.

routines.definition: things you do the same way at the same time regularly
routines.example: She follows the same morning routines every day.

tantrum.definition: when a child suddenly gets very angry and cries loudly
tantrum.example: The boy had a tantrum when he could not have candy.

argue.definition: to fight with words, to disagree strongly
argue.example: They would argue about where to eat dinner.

cajole.definition: to try to make someone do something by talking nicely or gently pushing them
cajole.example: She tried to cajole him into going to the party.

flexible.definition: able to change and accept different things easily
flexible.example: A flexible person can change plans without getting upset.

validating.definition: showing someone that their feelings are real and important
validating.example: Validating her sadness helped her feel understood.

crouch.definition: to bend your body low, close to the ground
crouch.example: He had to crouch down to talk to the small child.

subsided.definition: became less strong, quieter, or calmer
subsided.example: Her crying subsided after a few minutes.

stubborn.definition: refusing to change what you think or do
stubborn.example: The stubborn child would not eat her vegetables.

validated.definition: showed that someone's feelings are real and important
validated.example: He validated her feelings by listening carefully.

steering.example: She was steering the car carefully on the icy road.

skid.definition: when a car slides out of control on a wet or icy road
skid.example: The car went into a skid on the frozen road.

aligning.definition: putting things in the same line or direction
aligning.example: Aligning the wheels helped the car move straight.

regain.definition: to get something back that you lost
regain.example: She needed time to regain her strength after being sick.

acknowledge.definition: to accept that something is true or real
acknowledge.example: It is important to acknowledge how other people feel.

countering.definition: saying something against what another person said
countering.example: Instead of countering her, he just listened.

expense.definition: the cost or money spent on something
expense.example: The expense of the trip was more than they expected.

unappreciated.definition: not valued or noticed by others
unappreciated.example: She felt unappreciated after all her hard work.

alienate.definition: to make someone feel like they do not belong or are not welcome
alienate.example: Being too strict can alienate your children.

counter-intuitive.definition: the opposite of what you would naturally expect
counter-intuitive.example: It seems counter-intuitive, but sometimes doing less helps more.

exception.definition: something that does not follow the usual rule
exception.example: Children are no exception to this rule.

opportunity.definition: a chance to do something good
opportunity.example: This is a good opportunity to learn something new.

escalate.definition: to become worse or more serious
escalate.example: A small fight can quickly escalate into a big argument.

daft.example: Don't be daft, of course you can do it.

soothing.definition: making someone feel calm and less worried
soothing.example: Her soothing voice helped the baby stop crying.

unpleasant.example: Getting a shot at the doctor is unpleasant but necessary.

ashamed.definition: feeling bad about something you did or that happened to you
ashamed.example: She felt ashamed for making a mistake.

desperately.definition: in a very strong, urgent way because you need something badly
desperately.example: He desperately tried to finish the work before the deadline.

slip.definition: to make a small mistake without meaning to
slip.example: We all slip up sometimes when we are tired.

articulate.definition: to express a thought or feeling clearly in words
articulate.example: Young children cannot always articulate how they feel.

restless.definition: unable to stay still or be patient
restless.example: The children were restless after being inside all day.

cooped.definition: kept in a small space with no freedom to move
cooped.example: After being cooped up all day, she needed fresh air.

resonates.definition: feels true and meaningful to someone
resonates.example: His words resonated with everyone in the room.

connection.definition: a feeling of understanding and closeness with another person
connection.example: They felt a strong connection after their honest talk.

bored.definition: feeling tired and uninterested because nothing exciting is happening
bored.example: The children were bored on the rainy afternoon.

satisfied.definition: feeling that you have enough and are content
satisfied.example: She felt satisfied after the good conversation.

lonely.definition: feeling sad because you are alone or no one understands you
lonely.example: He felt lonely with his feelings because no one listened.

fuss.definition: showing too much worry or anger about something small
fuss.example: Don't make a fuss about a little scratch.

booster.definition: a special seat that makes a child sit higher in a car
booster.example: The little girl sat on the booster seat in the front.

strapped.definition: fastened tightly with a belt or strap
strapped.example: She strapped herself in before the car started moving.

chatting.definition: talking in a friendly, relaxed way
chatting.example: The girls were happily chatting about their weekend.

sighs.definition: breathes out slowly and heavily, usually showing sadness or tiredness
sighs.example: He sighs deeply whenever he thinks about the problem.

denying.definition: saying something is not true or not real
denying.example: Denying your child's feelings can hurt your relationship.

unhappiness.definition: the feeling of being sad or not happy
unhappiness.example: Denying unhappiness does not make it go away.

indoors.example: We stayed indoors because of the rain.

reality.definition: what is actually true, not what someone imagines
reality.example: The reality was different from what she expected.
